selection_rule: M3 recommends candidate components and weights from observed source evidence; L9 must approve or edit the policy before M2 executes it.

weight_recommendation_rule.ai_role: Recommend components, weights, and missing-component handling from L3 field evidence and retrieved metric templates.
weight_recommendation_rule.deterministic_test_fallback: Use default_weight_hint values as priors and renormalize over available components only when the configured AI model is not invoked in local tests.

component_registry.applicability_rule: product key and rating or review text evidence exist
rating field exists and rating scale is approved

component_registry.fallback_boundary: Local validation may use 1 - conversion_rate only to prove deterministic execution. Production policy should prefer an approved category/global baseline when one exists.

component_registry.applicability_rule: delivery lateness evidence and delivery denominator exist

required_inputs: component metric columns referenced by the approved policy

params_schema.risk_score_policy_ref: string artifact_id or versioned contract path

execution_semantics: Evaluate only components whose source metric is present and non-null for the row or product.
Renormalize approved weights over available components.
Return null when no approved component is available.
Write risk_score_coverage with component presence, weight_used, and missing reason.
block_conditions: approved weights do not sum to 1 after normalization
component references a missing metric without a missing-component rule

blocked_claims: Risk score formula is not globally fixed for every unknown dataset.
AI recommendation chooses candidate components and weights from available evidence.
Missing components are not treated as zero.
The local 1 - conversion_rate fallback is not a universal production risk formula.
